event: 66-year-old
Male
Hypertension
Hyperlipidemia
Hepatitis C virus infection
Atrial fibrillation
Chronically anticoagulated with apixaban
Brought in by emergency services after a fall
Reported dizziness
Lost consciousness
Found down by a family member
Glasgow Coma Score of 15
No focal neurological deficits
Noncontrast CT head showed 6 mm right frontal subdural hematoma (SDH) with no shift
Right medial orbital wall fracture
No operative intervention recommended
Admitted to surgical intensive care unit for serial checks
On the 2nd hospital day, developed severe headache
Repeat head CT showed worsening of SDH with mass effect and 2 mm midline shift
Remained neurologically intact
No surgical intervention recommended
Discharged to acute inpatient rehabilitation on the 6th hospital day
Instructions to repeat head CT in 2 weeks
In rehabilitation, 6 days after discharge, experienced a terrible headache for 3 days
Repeat CT head showed expansion of hematoma to 15 mm with 8 mm midline shift
Underwent burr holes for evacuation of the SDH
Placement of subdural drain
Headache improved after evacuation
SDH decreased in size on repeat CT head
Postoperative course without complication
Drain removed
Discharged home on the 2nd postoperative day
Started levetiracetam for seizure prophylaxis
On the 14th postoperative day, seen in office for follow-up
Described a strange sensation in the right arm
Felt that he could not control the arm
Sent directly to the emergency room
Developed a focal left arm seizure
Started on electroencephalography (EEG) monitoring
That night, found to be unresponsive
CT head showed no acute hemorrhage
EEG indicated status epilepticus
Intubated for airway protection
Transferred to medical intensive care unit
Cerebral CTA obtained
CTA negative for any pathology
Admission complicated by ventilator-dependent respiratory failure
Septic shock secondary to Pseudomonas bacteremia
Received a tracheostomy
Received a gastrostomy
Discharged to long-term acute care hospital on day 29
On levetiracetam for seizure control
On oxcarbazepine for seizure control
Four days after discharge, found to be less responsive
Transferred back for neurosurgical evaluation
CT and CTA head showed right frontal intraparenchymal hemorrhage measuring 5.2 cm×3.3 cm
No vascular lesion or anomaly on CTA
Cerebral catheter angiography showed arteriovenous malformation (AVM)
AVM was Spetzler-Martin Grade 1 (Spetzler-Ponce Grade A)
Embolization of the AVM performed using N-butyl-cyanoacrylate glue
Embolization successful with no opacification of early draining vein or AVM nidus
On the 8th day postembolization, taken to operating room for resection of the AVM
Right pterional craniotomy performed
Associated intraparenchymal hematoma was evacuated
Postoperative angiography showed no residual AVM
Mental status progressively improved postoperatively
Postoperative course complicated by pseudomonas sepsis
Treated in consultation with infectious disease
Discharged to long-term acute care on the 15th postoperative day
